steps.title: Isolate the hypothesis
steps.content: The researchers hypothesize that shoreline hardening harms waterbird communities more than land development does. Since a low IWCI score means low community integrity, support would come from a site with more hardening having a worse IWCI score than a site with more development but less hardening.

steps.title: Compare the site patterns
steps.content: Choice B gives the opposite result: the site with more hardening has the better IWCI score. Choices C and D change development and hardening together, so those comparisons cannot isolate which factor matters more. The remaining comparison separates the variables: Back River has more developed land and less hardening but a higher IWCI score than Magothy, which has less developed land and more hardening. The answer is A.
